response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web developer with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, and web platforms, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The two hardest requirements — native-level Japanese proficiency and deep C/Linux-kernel expertise for embedded networking — are both absent. His skill set is oriented entirely toward web, mobile, and application-layer software; there is no evidence of experience with network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), embedded firmware, L2/L3 networking, switch hardware, or any of the security domains the role requires.
strengths: Long, continuous software engineering career demonstrating broad technical adaptability across many languages and platforms
Some embedded systems exposure (robotic arms at Altom, low-latency systems at Sociomantic) and C/C++ listed in skills
Strong Linux familiarity and DevOps experience (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab) relevant to development environment setup
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is listed as a hard, non-negotiable requirement
No experience with any of the required security technologies: MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, tamper detection, or embedded firmware security
No Linux kernel development, kernel vulnerability research, or network switch/embedded hardware experience — the entire technical core of the role is missing

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
No Linux kernel or embedded C development
